back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'The Lily of the Covenant.' A Miktam of David for instruction.
When he fought Aram-naharaim and Aram-zobah, and Joab returned and struck down twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
You have rejected us, O God; You have broken us; You have been angry—restore us!
You have made the land quake and split it open; heal its fractures, for it is shaking.
You have shown Your people hardship; You have made us drink wine that makes us stagger.
You have given a banner to those who fear You, to be raised against the bow. Selah
That those You love may be delivered, answer us and save with Your right hand.
God has spoken in His holiness: 'In triumph I will parcel out Shechem and divide the Valley of Succoth.'
'Gilead is Mine, and Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim is the helmet for My head, Judah is My scepter.'
'Moab is My washbasin; upon Edom I cast My shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph.'
Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Is it not You, O God, who have rejected us, and no longer go out with our armies?
Give us help against the enemy, for the help of man is worthless.
Through God we will perform valiantly, and He will trample our enemies.
